rows.definition: The cmd/tokendemo served-read proof field (RawEngineCalls - FakEngineCalls) counting how many raw engine dispatches the kernel collapsed, alongside RawEngineCalls (the calls a stateless baseline would make), FakEngineCalls (the calls that actually ran the engine), EngineRanFak (the per-row flag that a call hit the real fak engine rather than the vDSO), and EngineDelayMs (the synthetic per-engine-call latency knob the timing proof injects).
rows.distinction: It is the demo-side ARITHMETIC of avoided dispatches over a replayed trace, distinct from the live coordinator-kernel counter EngineCalls that tallies real dispatches in flight.

rows.definition: The servewiring/serve-config seam (`inkernelchat`) by which a gateway started with --gguf/--tokenizer and no --base-url serves /v1/chat/completions and /v1/messages from the in-kernel model, built by loadServeInKernelModel (which also returns the InKernelQ4K resident-Q4_K flag) and run through NewInKernelPlanner's greedy decode.
rows.distinction: It is the CHAT-endpoint wiring that exposes the in-kernel model over HTTP, distinct from the InKernelPlanner that performs the actual in-process decode.

rows.definition: The abi.Event kind emitted when a tool call is served locally by the vDSO fast path (no adjudication, By="vdso"), counted distinctly by observers; it names the same fast-path serve that VDSOOffNet ablates away, that FromVDSOKey describes as a tier-2 tool-result cache entry, and that VDSOProxyFill warms from admitted inbound tool_result blocks.
rows.distinction: It is the EVENT signalling one vDSO serve, distinct from the cumulative VDSOHits counter that tallies how many such events fired.

rows.definition: The internal/model Session method (moe_offload.go) that selects which matKernel the GLM-DSA forward runs its dense GEMMs through in one place, where the underlying compute knobs include FAK_QKERNEL (the SIMD quant-GEMM tier pin), LinearConvKernelDim (the Gated-DeltaNet short-conv kernel width), the CUDA pure kernels in cuda_kernels.cu, and the worktype KernelOptimization throughput program.
rows.distinction: It is the GLM-DSA-specific COMPUTE-kernel selector inside the model forward, NOT the coordinator kernel (internal/kernel) that adjudicates and routes tool calls.

rows.definition: The Prometheus histogram of gateway operation latency labeled by operation type (adjudicate, syscall, admit), verdict, and deciding adjudicator, whose bucket cutoffs come from the package-level gatewayLatencyBuckets slice.
rows.distinction: It measures per-operation LATENCY, distinct from fak_gateway_operations_total which only counts adjudicated operations by verdict.

rows.definition: The gateway's witnessed KV-prefix reuse metric family (fak_gateway_kv_prefix_turns_total, _prompt_tokens_total, _reuse_ratio, _turns_by_regime_total) that measures realized RadixAttention prompt-prefix reuse per in-kernel turn, with fak_gateway_kv_prefix_reused_tokens_total as the specific counter of prefix tokens the kernel did NOT re-prefill.
rows.distinction: It measures fak's OWN realized prompt-prefix KV reuse, distinct from fak_gateway_operations_total which counts adjudicated kernel operations.

rows.definition: The gateway Server method that consults the optional per-call routing policy on every buildCall and returns the engine id to stamp onto abi.ToolCall.Engine (returning "" for the kernel-default path or an unset ensemble).
rows.distinction: It is the per-call SELECTION of which engine to dispatch to, distinct from EngineID which is the static registry key it returns and from the engine itself.

rows.definition: abi.EngineIDs() returns the id-sorted list of registered inference-engine drivers from the engine registry snapshot, used for forensics, gateway validation, and engine selection.
rows.distinction: It LISTS every registered engine id, distinct from a single EngineID (one registry key) and from the engines registry struct that holds the drivers.

rows.definition: The internal/engine SGLang adapter registered under the engine id "sglang" (SGLangEngineID), a lifecycle-capable EngineDriver in the dispatchable registry; the external vLLM/SGLang serve harness is configured separately through the ENGINE_ARGS env var passed to the serve script.
rows.distinction: It is one concrete REGISTERED engine driver (sglang), distinct from the abi engines registry that holds all drivers and from the generic EngineID key.

rows.definition: The cmd/fak test TestClaudeGLMGCPA100PlanWiresPureFakKernel, asserting the Claude-GLM-on-GCP-datacenter GPU plan wires the pure in-kernel fak model path (the DRY cuda-binary subcommand) rather than an external upstream.
rows.distinction: It is a TEST that the GCP plan wires the in-kernel model engine, distinct from fak_kernel_decisions_total (the rung-decision metric) and from the coordinator kernel itself.

rows.definition: The cmd/fak helper that validates and normalizes the gateway base URL a TUI agent / info command is pointed at, returning the canonical base used to build debug and agent requests.
rows.distinction: It NORMALIZES a gateway URL string for the TUI/agent client, distinct from the gateway subsystem it addresses and from the bearer-credential resolver defaultGatewayBearerToken.

rows.definition: The degenerate, never-wired engine.HTTPEngine OpenAI-client seam that was DELETED in v0.7.1 (with an architest invariant keeping the OpenAI client singular), leaving agent.HTTPPlanner as the one OpenAI client.
rows.distinction: It is the REMOVED duplicate engine HTTP seam, distinct from the live engine driver registry and the surviving single OpenAI planner.

rows.definition: The internal/model helper returning the GLM-DSA per-position key stride (NumHeads * (QKNopeHeadDim + QKRopeHeadDim)) used to size and index the K cache and paged GLM-DSA attention.
rows.distinction: It is the KEY-cache stride sizing for GLM-DSA's MLA attention, NOT the dense attention body attnSeq that runs the SDPA compute.

rows.definition: The internal/model Model method running the GLM-DSA MLA + dynamic-sparse whole-sequence attention sublayer, replicated across expert-parallel ranks and optionally sharing a top-k selection across layers.
rows.distinction: It is GLM-DSA's REPLICATED sparse-attention sequence body, NOT the standard dense softmax attention body attnSeq.

rows.definition: The HuggingFace config key `num_attention_heads` that benchcli/batchbench read into cfg.NumHeads (and from which HeadDim is derived as hidden_size/num_attention_heads when head_dim is absent).
rows.distinction: It is the COUNT of query attention heads from the model config, NOT the self_attn projection-weight namespace those heads use.
